title: Add on a number line
description: Practice adding numbers like 554 and 237 using a number line.  All numbers are less than 1000.

hints: $243 +\purpleE4\blueE1\maroonE2 = 243 +\purpleE{400}+\blueE{10}+\maroonE2$
On the number line, we start at $243$ and move **right** $\purpleE{400}$, then $\blueE{10}$, and finally $\maroonE2$ more.

The answer:

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Miriam thinks about $480+\blueE{159}$ as $480+\redE{20}+\purpleE{139}$ to solve in her head. Here is part of a number line to represent her thinking.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which number line correctly labels the other tick marks?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Every time we move right on the number line, we add the distance we move.

$480\redE{+20}=500$

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's keep adding to label the other points on the number line.

This leads to the same sum because, altogether, that is a distance of:

$\redE{20}+\purpleE{139}= \blueE{159}$

[[☃ image 1]]

This is Miriam's completed number line:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A number line with 3 unequally spaced tick marks. From left to right, the tick marks are labeled 480, an unlabeled tick mark, and an unlabeled tick mark. An arrow labeled plus 20 is above the number line beginning at 480 and ending at the first unlabeled point. An arrow labeled plus 139 is above the number line beginning at the first unlabeled point and ending at the second unlabeled point. An arrow labeled plus 159 is above the number line beginning at 480 and ending at the second unlabeled point.

choices.content: ![A number line with 3 unequally spaced tick marks. From left to right, the tick marks are labeled 480, 400, and 539. An arrow labeled plus 20 is above the number line beginning at 480 and ending at 400. An arrow labeled plus 139 is above the number line beginning at 400 and ending at 539. An arrow labeled plus 159 is above the number line beginning at 480 and ending at 539.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/504aafa7ca05fec20c631727d68ed651b4924594)

![A number line with 3 unequally spaced tick marks. From left to right, the tick marks are labeled 480, 500, and 639. An arrow labeled plus 20 is above the number line beginning at 480 and ending at 500. An arrow labeled plus 139 is above the number line beginning at 500 and ending at 639. An arrow labeled plus 159 is above the number line beginning at 480 and ending at 639.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f54fd44ce4ccdc370788f73ecf8027879ec42f7)

![A number line with 3 unequally spaced tick marks. From left to right, the tick marks are labeled 480, 489, and 539. An arrow labeled plus 20 is above the number line beginning at 480 and ending at 489. An arrow labeled plus 139 is above the number line beginning at 489 and ending at 539. assessment_items.question_content:
DeAndre thinks about $478+\blueE{363}$ as $478+\redE{2}+\purpleE{20}+\goldE{341}$ to solve in his head. Here is part of a number line to represent his thinking.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which number line correctly labels the other tick marks?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Every time we move right on the number line, we add the distance we move.

$478\redE{+2}=480$

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's keep adding to label the other points on the number line.

This leads to the same sum because, altogether, that is a distance of:

$\redE{2}+\purpleE{20}+\maroonE{341}= \blueE{363}$

[[☃ image 1]]

This is DeAndre's completed number line:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A number line with 4 unequally spaced tick marks. From left to right, the tick marks are labeled 478, an unlabeled tick mark, an unlabeled tick mark, and an unlabeled tick mark. An arrow labeled plus 2 is above the number line beginning at 478 and ending at the first unlabeled point. An arrow labeled plus 20 is above the number line beginning at the first unlabeled point and ending at the second unlabeled point. An arrow labeled plus 341 is above the number line beginning at the second unlabeled point and ending at the third unlabeled point. An arrow labeled plus 363 is above the number line beginning at 478 and ending at the third unlabeled point.

$571\redE{+90}=661$

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's keep adding to label the other points on the number line.

This leads to the same sum because, altogether, that is a distance of:

$\redE{90}+\purpleE{2}= \blueE{92}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Isi thinks about $718+\blueE{240}$ as $718+\redE{200}+\purpleE{40}$ to solve in their head. Here is part of a number line to represent their thinking.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which number line correctly labels the other tick marks?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Every time we move right on the number line, we add the distance we move.

$718\redE{+200}=918$

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's keep adding to label the other points on the number line.

This leads to the same sum because, altogether, that is a distance of:

$\redE{200}+\purpleE{40}= \blueE{240}$

[[☃ image 1]]

This is Isi's completed number line:

[[☃ image 1]]

An arrow labeled plus 40 is above the number line beginning at the first unlabeled point and ending at the second unlabeled point. An arrow labeled plus 240 is above the number line beginning at 718 and ending at the second unlabeled point.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression can we evaluate with the following number line?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
On the number line, we start at $832$ and move **right** $\blueE{70}$, then $\maroonE{9}$ more.

We can represent this as $832 +\blueE{70}+\maroonE{9}$.
$832 +\blueE{70}+\maroonE{9}=832 +\blueE7\maroonE{9}$
We can evaluate $832+79$ with the number line.

options.alt: A number line with 3 unequally spaced tick marks. From left to right, the tick marks are labeled 832, 902, and 911. An arrow labeled plus 70 is above the number line beginning at 832 and ending at 902. An arrow labeled plus 9 is above the number line beginning at 902 and ending at 911.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression can we evaluate with the following number line?**

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
On the number line, we start at $655$ and move **right** $\purpleE{100}$, then $\blueE{80}$, and finally $\maroonE4$ more.

We can represent this as $655 +\purpleE{100}+\blueE{80}+\maroonE4$.
$655 +\purpleE{100}+\blueE{80}+\maroonE4=655 +\purpleE1\blueE8\maroonE4$
We can evaluate $655+184$ with the number line.

options.alt: A number line with 4 unequally spaced tick marks. From left to right, the tick marks are labeled 655, 755, 835, and 839. An arrow labeled plus 100 is above the number line beginning at 655 and ending at 755. An arrow labeled plus 80 is above the number line beginning at 755 and ending at 835. An arrow labeled plus 4 is above the number line beginning at 835 and ending at 839.
